levels.name: Runner and artifact harness reliability
levels.claim: The runner emits deterministic diagnostics and versioned artifacts for cataloged, unsupported, Windows-only, and non-goal surfaces.

levels.name: Source-level app startup and XAML ingest
levels.claim: Public WinUI-style source projects can be ingested through the compat shadow build and launched as managed macOS processes for the documented subset.

levels.name: Supported controls render at usable harness quality
levels.claim: Supported and partial public component rows have recognizable source-level harness output with native reference provenance where visual.

levels.name: Interaction and accessibility evidence
levels.claim: Supported interactive controls expose deterministic script results and accessibility state for consumer CI assertions.

levels.name: State and theme coverage
levels.claim: Supported controls carry light, dark, high-contrast, disabled, focused, checked, selected, invalid, and popup-open evidence where applicable.

levels.name: Native-quality selected subset
levels.claim: Specific promoted component rows meet native-backed review gates; this level applies only to those rows, not to the whole renderer.

levels.name: Consumer package and release readiness
levels.claim: Package, quick-start, release candidate, privacy, benchmark, flake, and rollback evidence are present for a product release.

catalogDispositionMappings.evidenceRequirement: Tests, fixtures, macOS artifacts, native WinUI reference when visual, interaction/accessibility evidence where applicable, and docs.
Same source-level evidence bar as supported entries, plus exact partial-boundary wording and diagnostics for missing behavior.
Deterministic diagnostic, documentation, owner treatment, and promotion exit criteria.
Deterministic Windows-only exclusion, Windows validation where applicable, and support-policy wording.
Deterministic non-goal diagnostic and support-policy wording.
